body:
Subject: Re: Coffee maker still acting up

DeShawn,

Follow-up on that brew timer issue you walked me through Saturday. Reset it like you said, ran two cycles, and it's back to shutting off mid-brew around the 4-minute mark. Same behavior as before the reset.

Quick facts in case it helps:
- Machine: the one you recommended, had it about 3 weeks
- Outlet: kitchen counter, same circuit as the microwave, no overlap in usage
- Water reservoir descaled per manual, no change

Marcus Jr. has practice at 6am tomorrow so I need this thing reliable before then, not after. If it's a warranty issue just tell me straight and I'll deal with the return, don't need the optimistic spin.

Jay

Subject: Re: Coffee maker still acting up

Jay,

Mid-brew cutoff at a consistent 4 minutes points to the thermal fuse, not the timer. Resetting the timer wouldn't touch that, which is why nothing changed.

Do this before tomorrow's 6am wake-up call:
- Unplug for 10 minutes, full power drain, not just off
- Run one cycle with just water, no grounds, no filter
- If it still cuts at 4 minutes, it's the fuse and that's a warranty swap, not a fix

If that clean cycle finishes fine, it was a fluke and you're good. If not, box it up and I'll send you the RMA link tonight so you're not scrambling at 5am. Either way you're not going into practice on gas station coffee, I've seen that version of you and nobody needs it twice.

DeShawn

Subject: Re: Badge access still not fixed for server room

DeShawn,

Following up on the ticket from Monday. Badge still won't clear the server room door on the 3rd floor. I've been borrowing Priya's to get in, which isn't a long-term plan.

Can you check if the access group update actually went through on your end? I checked our AD group membership and it shows me added, but the reader must still be caching the old list.

Also — Marcus has a game Thursday evening, so if this needs an on-site fix, morning works better for me than end of day.

Jay

Jay,

Pulled the logs. The AD sync ran fine but the physical reader on 3rd floor didn't get the push — firmware on that panel's been flaky since the last building system update. I've queued a manual sync for tomorrow morning, should be in before you're at your desk.

If it's still dead by 10am, it's the reader itself and we'll need facilities to swap it, which is a whole different timeline. I'll loop you in either way so you're not stuck borrowing badges all week.

Noted on Thursday, I'll keep it to a morning fix if it comes to that.

DeShawn

DeShawn,

Confirmed, badge worked fine this morning. No more mooching off Priya.

Appreciate the quick turnaround, especially with the game schedule this week. Falcons practice ran long again so I owe you one anyway.

Still on for Friday night, by the way — I've got a controller with your name on it and a losing streak to fix.

Jay

Hey D,

Follow-up from Tuesday — you mentioned maybe swinging by Falcons practice to watch Marcus Jr. take some cuts before our gaming session. Coach moved us to the field behind Conard High this week, 6-7:30pm Thursday. Same lot, just enter off the north side or you'll end up circling for twenty minutes like last time.

Also still owe you a rematch after you ran the table Sunday. Bring snacks if you're coming to practice, kid's been asking about you.

- Jay

Jay,

North side entrance, got it. I'm in for Thursday, should be out of work by 5:30 so I'll head straight over. Snacks noted — I'll grab the good popsicles, not the ones that turn everyone's tongue blue for three days.

How's he doing with the batting stance you two were working on? Last time I saw him he was still stepping in the bucket a bit.

Also yes, rematch is happening, and yes, I'm bringing the same deck.

- D

D,

Stance is better. Coach had him doing dry swings against the fence all week and it's clicking — stopped bailing out on inside pitches Tuesday, actually squared one up. Small win but I'll take it.

Thursday works, just text when you're close so I know which lawn chair to save you from the Kowalski dad who talks through the whole practice.

Deck's fine, doesn't matter what you bring. See you Thursday.

- Jay

Jay,

Good, glad the fence drills paid off — tell him consistency beats power at his age, he'll get both eventually. I'll text when I'm five out. Do me a favor and don't let Kowalski's dad start in on travel ball again, I don't need the whole rundown twice.

Thursday, then game night after. I'll bring the deck and low expectations for you.

- D

DeShawn,

Thanks for covering the equipment bag drop-off at practice yesterday, saved me a scramble. Marcus Jr. said the new drill you ran for infield footwork actually clicked for him, first time all season he wasn't tripping over second base.

Quick ask: are you still good for Thursday's practice? I've got a late meeting at Ironclad that might run long, and Patricia's got a work thing too, so if you can hold down the fort for the first 20 minutes that'd help a lot. I'll head straight over after.

Also, coach wants to know if you're still in for the scrimmage Saturday. Let me know so I can send the lineup.

-Jay

Jay,

Yeah, I got Thursday covered, go handle your meeting. Glad the footwork drill landed, that's the one my nephew's coach used on him, figured it was worth stealing.

Saturday I'm in, just tell me what time and if I need to bring anything besides my own aging knees. Also tell Marcus Jr. he owes me a rematch on the video game, he got lucky last time and he knows it.

See you Thursday.

DeShawn

Hey Deshawn,

Following up on the Google Calendar API thing I mentioned Sunday during the gaming session. I finally got a repro at work. We've got a small internal tool that syncs shift schedules to shared calendars for the claims team, and the OAuth refresh token is dying after about a week. Users have to re-auth, which for 40+ adjusters is a support headache I don't need on top of everything else.

I checked the obvious stuff - scopes look right (calendar.events), redirect URI matches what's registered in the console, and the refresh_token is actually coming back on the initial grant. It just stops working silently after some days. No error in our logs, calls just start returning 401.

Can you take a look when you get a chance? I know this isn't really your job title but you've debugged worse for me before. Also still need to nail down practice times for the Falcons this week if Coach moves us to Wednesday again.

- Jay

Jay,

Got it, pulled up what you sent. 401 with no error detail after silent expiry is almost always one of two things: the refresh token got revoked because the OAuth consent screen is still in Testing mode (Google caps those tokens at 7 days), or a second client somewhere is requesting a new refresh token for the same user and invalidating the old one under the hood.

Check your OAuth consent screen status in Cloud Console first - if it says "Testing," that's your answer, the 7-day expiry matches your timeline exactly. Moving it to "In Production" (needs verification if you're using sensitive scopes, but calendar.events might not need full verification) fixes it permanently.

If it's already in production, then look for anywhere else in your codebase requesting auth with prompt=consent - that reissues tokens and kills the old ones. Grep for "access_type=offline" across your services, bet you've got two places calling it.

Let me know what you find. Also yeah, Wednesday again, Coach really loves moving practice on us. I'll bring the good radar gun so Marcus can see his velo numbers, he was asking last time.

-D

Deshawn,

That was it. Consent screen was sitting in Testing, nobody had bothered to push it to production since whoever built this left two years ago. Filed the verification request this morning, scopes we use are non-sensitive so it should clear without the whole song and dance.

Ran a manual token refresh test after flipping the setting and it's holding steady past the old 7-day mark, so calling this closed pending the verification going through. Appreciate the fast turnaround, saved me a very annoying recurring ticket queue.

Marcus is going to lose it over the radar gun, he's been talking about it all week. See you and the guys at practice, and I owe you a game later - lost track of how many rounds you're up on me at this point.

- Jay

Hey D,

Following up on the Gmail API thing I mentioned last week. I got the OAuth consent screen set up on our Ironclad test project, but I'm still getting a 403 invalid_scope error when I try to pull message metadata. Scopes look right in the code but Google's console shows the app as "testing" status with only 3 allowed test users listed, and mine isn't one of them apparently.

Can you walk me through adding test users again? I know you did this for the calendar API thing back in March but I didn't save notes like an idiot.

Also — Marcus has a game Thursday evening so if you want to do the screen share, mornings work better for me this week.

- Jay

Jay,

Yeah, that's almost always the test user list. Go to APIs & Services > OAuth consent screen > Test users > Add Users, drop your Ironclad email in there, save, then wait about 5 min for it to propagate before retesting. If it still 403s after that, double check the scope string itself — gmail.metadata vs gmail.readonly is a common typo and they are NOT interchangeable, Google will not warn you nicely about it.

Morning works, I'll be up early anyway, been queuing for ranked at like 6am lately. Let's do 8am Tuesday before you're in the office.

Send me the error log if it's still broken after adding the test user, easier than guessing.

- D

D,

Added the test user, waited it out, still 403. Pulled the log — turns out I had gmail.readonly in the manifest but was requesting gmail.metadata in the actual auth call. Fixed the mismatch and it authenticated fine. Should've listened to you the first time.

Now I'm hitting a rate limit issue instead, getting 429s after maybe 50 requests in a batch job. I know there's a quota page in the console but the numbers on it don't match what I'm seeing in practice. Figured you'd know if there's a smarter way to batch these than firing them one at a time.

Tuesday 8am still works. I'll have coffee ready, don't be late this time.

- Jay

Jay,

Glad the scope thing sorted itself out, told you. For the 429s — you want exponential backoff, and honestly just use the batch endpoint (users.messages.batch) instead of looping individual calls, cuts your request count down a lot. Google's quota page lags the real-time counter by a bit so don't trust it during a live test, just watch the response headers instead.

I'll send you a small snippet Tuesday that handles the backoff/retry logic, used it on a project at work and it's held up fine.

See you at 8, I'll bring the good creamer since your coffee setup is questionable at best.

- D

Hey DeShawn,

Following up on ticket #4471 from last week — the Google Drive API quota errors on our document ingestion service account. Still seeing 403 rateLimitExceeded on batch jobs after 2pm, right around when the underwriting team kicks off their EOD sync. Wanted to check if the quota bump ever went through on your end, or if it's stuck in review somewhere.

Also, did the domain-wide delegation scope change get applied to the new service account (ironclad-drive-sync@)? I don't want to redeploy the connector until I know that's live, otherwise I'm just recreating the same mess.

No rush on a call, email's fine. Let me know if you need me to pull fresh error logs — I can grab a clean batch tomorrow morning before the 9am standup.

Talk later — assuming you're still showing up Friday for the gaming session, in which case I expect this ticket closed by Thursday.

Jay

Jay,

Good timing — quota increase came through this morning, bumped you to 20,000 queries/100 seconds per user on the Drive API. Should clear up the 403s during the afternoon sync window. Let me know if you're still seeing throttling after a redeploy.

On delegation: yes, ironclad-drive-sync@ has the scope added (drive.readonly + drive.metadata.readonly) and it's propagated on Google's end as of about an hour ago. You're clear to point the connector at it. If auth still fails, it's probably a stale token cache on your side, not the scope.

Send me the fresh logs if the errors persist past tomorrow's sync — don't need them otherwise, no sense digging through noise if the quota fix already handles it.

And yeah, I'll be there Friday. Ticket's closed either way, don't get cute.

DeShawn

Hey D,

Following up on the ticket from Tuesday — the Discord bot integration for the Falcons practice-reminder channel is still throwing 429s during peak evening hours. I throttled our request rate on our end like you suggested, batching the roster pings instead of firing them individually, but it's still choking around 6pm when everyone's checking in before practice.

Can you check if our app got flagged for rate limit review on your side? I pulled the response headers and retry-after is creeping up over the last week, not staying flat like it should.

Also — separate from the ticket — you still on for Friday night session? Marcus has a game that afternoon so I'll be a little late logging on.

Jay

Jay,

Good catch on the retry-after creeping up — pulled your app ID and yeah, it looks like you're hitting the global rate limit bucket, not a per-route one. That happens when requests aren't including the right major param in the route (channel ID in this case), so Discord is bucketing everything together instead of splitting it out.

Quick fix: make sure your batched roster pings are still keyed per-channel in the request path, not just batched in your own queue. If you're using a wrapper library, check it's not defaulting to a shared bucket handler. Send me the library/version you're on and I can point you to the exact config flag.

In the meantime, add a jitter of 200-500ms between batch sends so you're not slamming the bucket edge at exactly :00 and :30 — that's probably why it spikes right at 6pm on the dot.

Still good for Friday. Figured you'd be late, I'll queue up something we can drop in and out of.

D

D,

That was it. We were on a wrapper that defaulted to a global bucket handler — flipped the per-channel flag, added the jitter, and the 429s are gone. Ran clean through practice reminders last night with zero retries.

Appreciate you digging into the headers instead of just telling me to "try again later" like the last vendor did. Go ahead and close the ticket out.

See you Friday — bring the good headset this time, mine's been cutting out and I'm blaming Marcus's Xbox for the interference even though I have no evidence.

Jay

DeShawn,

Follow-up on the coffee maker thing I mentioned before Little League practice. Ran the descale cycle twice with the vinegar solution you suggested and it's still spitting out that first cup lukewarm before it hits temp. Grind's fine, water reservoir's clean, I'm stumped.

Before I sink more time into it I wanted to check if this is the same fix you did for yours last year or if I'm about to buy a new machine for no reason. Practice starts at 6 so I've got maybe 20 minutes tomorrow morning to troubleshoot before I'm out the door.

Let me know what you did.

-Jay

Jay,

Yeah, same symptom I had. Nine times out of ten it's the thermostat sensor being slow to kick on, not the heating element itself. Check if the machine has a "pre-infusion" or warm-up setting buried in a menu somewhere — mine had it disabled by default and turning it on fixed the lukewarm-first-cup thing completely.

If there's no such setting, it might just be scale buildup in the internal tubing that vinegar isn't reaching. Might need the actual descaling tablets, not vinegar, to break it up.

Let me know if the setting exists, otherwise I'll send you a link to what I used.

-D

DeShawn,

Found it. There was a "boost" mode buried three menus deep, no idea why that's not just on by default. Turned it on last night, ran it this morning before practice, first cup was actually hot for once.

Marcus asked why I was talking to the coffee maker at 5:45am. Fair question.

Appreciate the help, saved me from buying a new machine I didn't need.

-Jay

Jay,

Glad it was that simple. Manufacturers bury the one setting that actually matters, every time.

Still on for gaming this week? Figured we could run a few rounds and you can tell me more about the coffee maker's personality since apparently it needs talking to now.

Tell Marcus I said good game if practice went well.

-D

Hey D,

Following up on the ticket I opened yesterday — the VPN client issue on the west building floor 3 switches. Still getting drop-offs every 20 minutes or so, which is fun when you're mid-deploy. I'm in-office at Ironclad all week so if you need remote hands or console access, I'm right here.

Any update on the firmware push you mentioned? Want to get ahead of it before I head out early Thursday for Marcus's Little League practice — coach benches you if you're late, apparently, doesn't matter if you're the parent or the kid.

Also still owe you a rematch Friday night. Don't think I forgot.

- Jay

Jay,

Good timing — I just pushed the firmware to a test switch on floor 3 this morning, no drops in the last four hours, which is the longest it's gone without falling over. Going to stage it for the rest of the closet tonight during the maintenance window so it doesn't touch your afternoon.

I'll ticket you once it's done and you can hammer on it tomorrow before you cut out for practice. No promises the switches respect Little League schedules though.

Friday's on. I've got a new loadout and zero mercy.

- D

Subject: Falcons practice — Tuesday move

DeShawn,

Coach wants to shift Tuesday's practice to 5:30 since the field's double-booked with the rec league. Can you still make it, or is that too close to when you get off?

Marcus has been working on his glove-side pickups since last week and actually wants extra reps, so I'm bringing a bucket of balls early if you want to help me run a station.

Let me know by tonight so I can tell the group chat.

Jay

Subject: Re: Falcons practice — Tuesday move

Jay,

5:30 works, I'll cut out a little early. Tell Marcus his glove-side pickups better be worth the field time, last week he was still catching them like they owed him money.

I can run the station, just point me at whatever drill you've got planned. I'll bring the extra cones too since ours are looking rough.

See you Tuesday.

DeShawn

Subject: Re: Falcons practice — Tuesday move

DeShawn,

Good, appreciate it. Cones are appreciated, ours have been missing since the last thunderstorm blew half of them into the parking lot.

Marcus is already talking trash about his glove side, so consider yourself warned. Patricia's dropping him off, I'll grab you a coffee on the way in since you're doing me a favor.

Still on for the usual gaming session after, assuming nobody's kid needs a ride home first.

Jay

Hey D,

Follow-up from Tuesday — you mentioned maybe swinging by Falcons practice to watch Marcus Jr. take some cuts before our gaming session. Coach moved us to the field behind Conard High this week, 6-7:30pm Thursday. Same lot, just enter off the north side or you'll end up circling for twenty minutes like last time.

Also still owe you a rematch after you ran the table Sunday. Bring snacks if you're coming to practice, kid's been asking about you.

- Jay

Jay,

North side entrance, got it. I'm in for Thursday, should be out of work by 5:30 so I'll head straight over. Snacks noted — I'll grab the good popsicles, not the ones that turn everyone's tongue blue for three days.

How's he doing with the batting stance you two were working on? Last time I saw him he was still stepping in the bucket a bit.

Also yes, rematch is happening, and yes, I'm bringing the same deck.

- D

D,

Stance is better. Coach had him doing dry swings against the fence all week and it's clicking — stopped bailing out on inside pitches Tuesday, actually squared one up. Small win but I'll take it.

Thursday works, just text when you're close so I know which lawn chair to save you from the Kowalski dad who talks through the whole practice.

Deck's fine, doesn't matter what you bring. See you Thursday.

- Jay

Jay,

Good, glad the fence drills paid off — tell him consistency beats power at his age, he'll get both eventually. I'll text when I'm five out. Do me a favor and don't let Kowalski's dad start in on travel ball again, I don't need the whole rundown twice.

Thursday, then game night after. I'll bring the deck and low expectations for you.

- D
